purpose: Catalog-forced twin of the paper-3 anchored-H0 + survey-b_pred pipeline: fix the void history to the telescope-measured below-mean f_v^obs(z) and rerun both legs. Concrete experiment required by the paper-3 symmetry rule.

fvobs_history.definition: f_v^obs(z) = Phi(sigma0*D(z)/2), sigma0=0.734480 (2M++ 4 Mpc/h below-mean anchor), flat-LCDM growth D(z), Om=0.315.
fvobs_history.declared_systematic: z={1.3,2.33} EXTRAPOLATE the below-mean floor Phi(sigma0*D(z)/2) beyond the BOSS reach (z<~0.7); both stay >=0.5 by the floor theorem. Carried as a declared systematic. z={0,0.3,0.7} are the committed telescope PRIMARY nodes.

fullrate_shape_robust_check.note: anchored FULL-RATE H0 ~ 70 for the forced f_v^obs vs 73.3 for the fitted V: the SH0ES ladder measures the ~shape-robust LOCAL Hubble slope, so both land near the SH0ES value, but the flat f_v^obs fits the low-z Hubble diagram slightly worse (anchored chi2/dof 1.18 vs fitted 0.88), pulling the anchored local rate ~3 below the fitted value. The residual is small vs the local-minus-global gap: even the forced ~70 towers over the forced GLOBAL full-rate 52.98.

lcdm_validation_gate.expected: ~73.5 (71.5-75); confirms the GLS anchoring machinery is intact under the forced shape.

global_joint_fit.source: paper-2 forced_joint_fit.json (SN+BAO+CMB, DESI DR2 headline; f_v^obs FIXED, 0 shape params)
global_joint_fit.n_fitted_params_note: SN offset + BAO alpha profiled; ZERO fitted cosmological shape params (f_v FIXED)
global_joint_fit.note: The forced f_v^obs joint fit is CATASTROPHICALLY bad: chi2/dof=1.787 (~22 sigma above its dof), vs LCDM chi2/dof=0.880. It misses the (most-generous, one-fewer-parameter) BIC bar by 1438 chi2 -> paper-2 verdict FORCED_FVOBS_FAILS_BIC_BAR. This is why the global Hbar0 lands LOW (50.82 vs fitted 53.79): the flat high-z tail cannot bend the SN+BAO+CMB Hubble diagram.

b_req_obs.formula: anchored bare Hbar0 / global bare Hbar0 - 1
b_req_obs.note: b_req^obs is convention-independent (same fixed shape -> same g_dress, Hd0; reduces to the Hbar0 ratio). It is LARGER than the fitted b_req=0.08417 because the forced global fit craters to a lower Hbar0 -- i.e. the required local bias is even bigger for the observed shape, which the mechanism (part b) fails to supply by an even wider margin.

E_max_obs.fvprime0_note: df_v/dtau at z=0 (=2*gamma_bar_dot). This LOW-z slope, not the high-z tail, sets E_max; it is ~5x smaller for the flat f_v^obs than for the steep fitted V, which is why E_max and b_pred_survey drop ~5x. E_max->0 as the history flattens.
E_max_obs.formula: E_max = gamma_bar0*H_v0/Hdress0 - 1 (void-scale apparent-H0 maximum, pure clock conv.)

vs_fitted.note: E_max(f_v^obs) < fitted 0.33650: f_v^obs is FLATTER at low z, so |f_v'(0)| (hence the void-growth term driving the apparent-H0 maximum) is smaller. E_max=0 for a perfectly flat history; f_v^obs sits near that limit.

survey_geometry.note: identical SH0ES geometry to the fitted WP-H2' run; only E_max changes with the shape.

b_pred_survey.definition: b_pred_survey = E_max * <phi>_Hubble-flow (LA/obs, raised_cosine, r_void=30, r_hom=100, zCMB)

vs_fitted.note: b_pred_survey(f_v^obs)=0.0047 is ~5x SMALLER than the fitted 0.024 -- both are set by the low-z slope |f_v'(0)|, which is ~5x smaller for the flat f_v^obs. Both are far below b_req (0.084): the mechanism FAILS for either shape, harder for the observed one.

comparison_to_b_req.note: b_pred_survey UNDER-predicts b_req across the ENTIRE phi-shape band against BOTH the fitted requirement (0.08417) and the catalog-forced requirement b_req^obs. FAILS.

low_z_slope.note: |df_v/dz| near z=0 is ~5x smaller for f_v^obs -> ~5x smaller E_max/b_pred.

where_shapes_differ.note: The two shapes AGREE in VALUE at z=0 (both ~0.64) but differ everywhere else: f_v^obs is far FLATTER in slope from the start (|f_v'(0)| ~5x smaller) and diverges enormously at high z (fitted V plunges to 0.194 by z=2.33 while f_v^obs stays at 0.555 on the below-mean floor). The z=0 VALUE fixes g_dress (identical); the low-z SLOPE sets Hd0 and E_max; the high-z TAIL sets the joint-fit quality. The SH0ES low-z ladder pins the measured local RATE ~shape-robustly (~70-73; the model absorbs the slope difference into the Hbar0/Hd0 split), but E_max -- the slope-driven void-growth term -- drops ~5x, and the joint fit craters on the flat high-z tail.

finding: The catalog-forced mechanism FAILS identically to the fitted case. Substituting the exact survey-measured void history f_v^obs(z) for the joint-fit winner V and rerunning BOTH legs: (a) the anchored FULL-RATE H0 barely moves (69.91 vs fitted 73.3) -- the SH0ES low-z ladder pins the measured local rate ~shape-robustly (the model absorbs the shape difference into the Hbar0/Hd0 split; anchored chi2/dof 1.18 vs fitted 0.88). But the global joint SN+BAO+CMB fit of the flat f_v^obs is CATASTROPHIC (chi2/dof=1.79, ~22 sigma, misses the BIC bar by 1438) and craters to a low global Hbar0=50.82, so b_req^obs=0.320 (>> the fitted 0.08417). (b) the survey b_pred DROPS ~5x: E_max(f_v^obs)=0.0657 << fitted 0.336 and b_pred_survey=0.0047 << fitted 0.024 (both set by |f_v'(0)|, ~5x smaller for the flat shape) -- so it FAILS HARDER (17.9x below the fitted b_req, 68x below b_req^obs). FINDING: the fitted and observed shapes agree in VALUE at z=0 (fv0~0.64) but the fitted V is far steeper and plunges to 0.19 by z=2.33 while f_v^obs stays at 0.56 on the below-mean floor. That high-z divergence is nearly INVISIBLE to the low-z SH0ES ladder (anchored H0 ~70 either way) yet FATAL to the joint fit (global Hbar0 craters); and the low-z survey-averaged b_pred cannot supply b_req for EITHER shape (0.024 and 0.0047, both far below 0.084). The mechanism fails identically whether the void structure is FITTED to the Hubble diagram or TELESCOPE-supplied: b_pred_survey stays an order of magnitude below b_req in both.
